rootCause: Client address create and update trusted client-supplied serviceAreaCode and coordinates after UI verification, leaving a direct API bypass around governed DSH geofences and serviceability truth.
implemented: Backend resolves submitted coordinates through the DSH service-area resolver and requires the resolved active code to match serviceAreaCode.
Create idempotency replay is checked before fresh geofence verification so a previously committed request remains replayable after geofence changes.
PostgreSQL trigger rejects missing coordinates, inactive geofences, mismatched codes, outside-polygon writes, and race/direct-database bypasses.
Soft deletion remains permitted so privacy anonymization and retention workflows can operate.
OpenAPI documents required coordinates and HTTP 422 ADDRESS_SERVICE_AREA_UNVERIFIED behavior.
Dedicated JRN-006 CI publishes an exact-commit status and artifact.

scopeNotes.jrn006: PASS — evidence_scope: static, Go ownership packages, PostgreSQL 16 migration and invariant behavior
scopeNotes.aggregateWorkflow: The aggregate JRN-001..010 status was failure because of unrelated concurrent failures outside JRN-006; the dedicated JRN-006 status was success on the same commit.
